evidence: All 10 toolkit tools consistently return Promise<ToolResult<T>> via withToolWrapper — error wrapping, timing, and observer notification are uniform
connect/disconnect explicitly document their different error contract (throw/void vs ToolResult) in the docstring
SdkBridge interface and implementation match — 15+ methods all honor their declared signatures
types.ts docstring says '9 error codes' but DemosErrorCode has 10 members (PARTIAL_SUCCESS was added)

createLifecyclePlugin() returns hooks: {} but the actual hook (lifecycleBeforeSense) is exported separately and wired via extensions.ts — the FrameworkPlugin contract is technically honored since hooks is optional, but misleading
verify.ts correctly handles all edge cases: null (unsupported), confirmed, unconfirmed, error — return type is always ToolResult<VerifyResult>
attestDahr returns DahrResult with responseHash/txHash/data/url — all fields always populated (responseHash may be empty string)
contract_coherence.issues_preventing_higher_score: Docstring on types.ts is stale (says 9 codes, has 10). createLifecyclePlugin() returns empty hooks which is misleading. attestDahr responseHash can be empty string which is surprising for a required field.

contract_coherence.dimension_character: The toolkit layer demonstrates strong contract coherence. All 10 tools consistently return ToolResult<T> via withToolWrapper, connect/disconnect clearly document their throw/void exception, and the SdkBridge interface faithfully mirrors its implementation. The few contract issues are minor: a stale docstring mentioning 9 error codes when 10 exist, and createLifecyclePlugin returning an empty hooks object while the actual hook is separately exported. A developer working here would generally trust that functions do what they promise.
contract_coherence.score_rationale: This scores above the 80 anchor because the core typed contracts (ToolResult, DemosError, SdkBridge) are consistently honored across the codebase. The defects are isolated and low-impact — a stale docstring count and a misleading empty-hooks plugin factory. These pull the score slightly below 90 because they require a developer to look past surface-level declarations, but they don't undermine the fundamental reliability of the contracts.

issues.summary: types.ts docstring says '9 error codes' but DemosErrorCode has 10 members
evidence: Line 4: docstring says 'DemosError: typed error union with 9 error codes'
Lines 14-24: DemosErrorCode has 10 members including PARTIAL_SUCCESS (likely added after the docstring was written)
issues.suggestion: Update the docstring to say '10 error codes' or remove the count entirely to avoid future staleness.

issues.summary: createLifecyclePlugin() returns hooks: {} while actual hook is exported separately
evidence: Line 93: createLifecyclePlugin returns hooks: {} — suggests no hooks
Line 23: lifecycleBeforeSense is exported as a standalone function
extensions.ts line 214-216: dynamically imports and wires lifecycleBeforeSense bypassing the FrameworkPlugin hooks contract
issues.suggestion: Either wire the hook into the plugin's hooks map (`hooks: { beforeSense: lifecycleBeforeSense }`) so the plugin is self-describing, or remove createLifecyclePlugin() since the hook is wired via the extension system anyway.

issues.summary: attestDahr can return empty string for responseHash — surprising for a required field
evidence: Line 525: `responseHash: String(proxyResponse.responseHash ?? '')` — empty string is a valid return
DahrResult interface declares responseHash: string (required, not optional)
Callers checking `result.responseHash` for truthiness will silently skip empty strings
issues.suggestion: Throw an error when responseHash is missing from the proxy response (the attestation failed if there is no hash), or make the field optional (`responseHash?: string`) to signal that absence is expected.

root_causes: Minor docstring/interface drift during iterative development — the underlying typed contracts are sound

add.description: All 10 toolkit tools return ToolResult<T> via withToolWrapper. Error wrapping, timing, and observer notification are uniform. connect/disconnect explicitly document their throw/void exception.

The 15+ method SdkBridge interface faithfully mirrors the createSdkBridge factory's implementation. Return types, error behaviors, and optional fields are consistent between interface and implementation.
Lifecycle plugin has both a FrameworkPlugin factory (empty hooks) and a standalone hook export wired via extensions.ts. The two paths are not connected — the factory is misleading.
